us.headline: Tariff stacks and origin rules — not mill prices — drive your landed cost.

regulation.b: China-origin goods carry additive Section 301 duties (many lists 25%; consumer List 4A 7.5%). A new 12.5% forced-labor 301 tariff was proposed on 2 Jun 2026 and is under review. Some agricultural equipment has a temporary reduced 15% compound rate. Country of origin materially changes landed cost.
Duties compose additively (MFN + Section 301 + any Section 232 on steel/aluminium). Confirm each product's HTS code before costing.

us.supply: Asia–US (transpacific) spot rates eased in late June 2026 (≈ US$3,200/FEU West Coast, ≈ US$5,000 East Coast) — a window to lock favourable contract rates. Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly.
us.view: US landed cost is dominated by tariffs and origin, not spot material prices. Birdland's two-country production (Taiwan + China) lets you shift origin to manage tariff exposure, and we prepare clear origin documentation. When freight or materials dip, the smart move is to lock contract rates and supply — our pricing reflects secured capacity and compliant origin, not a single day's index.

ca.headline: ‘Country of melt’ now decides whether China steel surtaxes hit you.

regulation.b: Since 31 Jul 2025 Canada applies a 25% surtax on steel melted & poured (and aluminium cast) in China, on top of normal duties. From 26 Dec 2025 a 25% tariff also covers listed steel-derivative products — some with only partial steel content.
Several temporary remissions end 1 Jul 2026. Confirm classification and country of melt before shipping.

ca.supply: Landed steel cost into Canada is shaped more by origin-of-melt surtaxes than by mill prices. Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly.
ca.view: Birdland can source Taiwan-melted steel content with clear traceability, helping you stay outside the China-melt surtax, and we document origin end-to-end. The driver here is provenance and paperwork, not spot steel — our quotes reflect verified origin and stable supply.

au.headline: Timber biosecurity and legal-logging compliance gate every wood-handled tool.

regulation.b: Wood-handled goods must meet DAFF biosecurity conditions — bark-free, pest-free, treated/certified — checked against the BICON database.
The Illegal Logging Prohibition Act requires due diligence on timber, and wood packaging must meet ISPM 15. Phytosanitary certificates have been electronic (ePhyto) since Aug 2025.

au.supply: Compliance and treatment readiness, more than freight price, determine clearance speed into Australia. Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly.
au.view: Birdland supplies treated, certified, documented timber handles and ISPM 15-compliant packaging, so shipments clear cleanly. The value we sell is documented compliance and reliability — not the lowest spot timber cost.

uk.headline: Britain is diverging from the EU — plan to GB rules separately.

regulation.b: Great Britain sits outside the EU's EUDR and CBAM. The UK is developing its own forest-risk due-diligence rules, and a UK carbon border levy is planned (reportedly from 2027). Treat GB requirements separately from the EU.
UK product-marking and conformity rules still apply alongside EU rules for dual-market ranges — confirm marking per market.

uk.supply: Asia–Europe lanes are firming into the 2026 peak season (book early). Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly.
uk.view: Selling into both the EU and GB means two rulebooks. Birdland prepares documentation for each destination and keeps origin traceable, so a dual-market range ships without surprises. Our pricing reflects this compliance work and secured supply.

me.headline: Energy and shipping volatility is the main planning risk.

regulation.b: Gulf and wider Middle East markets use national/GCC conformity schemes (for example SASO-type approvals) that differ by country — confirm marking, certificates and labelling per destination.

me.supply: Strait-of-Hormuz tension kept Brent near US$105/bbl in mid-2026 with sharp swings, feeding fuel and freight volatility. Booking freight ahead buffers the swings.
me.view: In a volatile-energy region, predictability is the product. Birdland books freight early and holds key materials to cushion swings, and prepares per-market certificates. When oil eases, the right move is to lock rates — our quotes reflect secured supply, not a single day's price.

sa.headline: Duties, currency and documentation vary widely — plan per country.

regulation.b: Import duties and paperwork differ sharply across the region (Mercosur members vs others), and some markets apply anti-dumping measures. Confirm tariff classification and required certificates per country.

sa.supply: Currency volatility can move landed cost as much as freight or materials do. Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly.
sa.view: Birdland plans shipments and pricing to cushion FX and duty swings, and provides clear origin documentation to speed customs. The value is steady, well-documented supply — not chasing the cheapest spot input.

global.headline: Three forces shape 2026: tighter EU compliance, tariff/origin rules, and freight + energy volatility.

regulation.b: The EU Deforestation Regulation applies from 30 Dec 2026 for large/medium operators (small & micro to 30 Jun 2027). Wood-handled tools need geolocation data and proof of legal, deforestation-free timber. In May 2026 the Commission confirmed it will not reopen the text — prepare documentation now.

regulation.t: CBAM — carbon cost on steel content
regulation.b: CBAM entered its definitive phase on 1 Jan 2026; financial obligations on embedded carbon in imported steel now apply, with the first certificate surrender in 2027. Steel-content items (blades, frames) face added landed cost and reporting.
US Section 301 stacks and a proposed new 12.5% tariff (under review, Jun 2026), plus Canada's 25% surtax on China-melted steel, mean country of origin increasingly decides landed cost.

global.supply: Asia–Europe freight is firming into peak season while transpacific eased late-June 2026; Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly.
global.view: Across every market the pattern is the same: compliance documentation and origin now matter more than spot prices. Birdland's two-country production, early freight booking, material locking and full compliance paperwork (EUDR, CBAM data, ISPM 15, origin) keep you a season ahead. Our quotes reflect secured materials, compliant documentation and reliable supply — when an index dips, that stability is exactly what protects your margin.

nl.headline: EU rules tighten on timber, steel carbon and chemicals — documentation is now the deciding factor.

regulation.b: The EU Deforestation Regulation applies from 30 Dec 2026 for large/medium operators (small & micro to 30 Jun 2027). Wood-handled tools need geolocation data and proof of legal, deforestation-free timber. In May 2026 the Commission confirmed it will not reopen the text — prepare documentation now.

regulation.t: CBAM — carbon cost on steel content
regulation.b: CBAM entered its definitive phase on 1 Jan 2026; financial obligations on embedded carbon in imported steel now apply, with the first certificate surrender in 2027. Steel-content items (blades, frames) face added landed cost and reporting.

ECHA's final PFAS consultation closed 25 May 2026; SEAC's final opinion is due end-2026 and adoption is expected in 2027. May affect fluorinated coatings and finishes — review handle/blade coatings early.

nl.supply: Asia–Europe ocean freight is firming into the 2026 peak season (Shanghai–Rotterdam ≈ US$2,773/FEU, +15% w/w in early June), and Brent crude hovered around US$105/bbl in mid-2026 amid Strait-of-Hormuz tension, with high volatility; resin and energy-linked input costs can swing quickly. CBAM carbon charges lift steel landed costs regardless of mill prices.
nl.view: As an EU destination, the Netherlands rewards suppliers who arrive compliance-ready. Birdland prepares EUDR geolocation and legal-timber evidence, supports CBAM carbon data on steel parts, and reviews coatings against PFAS — while booking freight early and locking key materials. When costs dip, the honest takeaway is not “cut price” but “lock stability before the next swing”: our quotes reflect secured materials, compliant documentation and reliable supply, not yesterday's spot price.

teamAnalysis: Compliance and origin — not spot prices — are setting landed cost into 2026. EU buyers face EUDR, CBAM and PFAS tightening together; US and Canadian cost is driven by tariff stacks and country-of-origin/melt rules; freight is firming into the Asia–Europe peak while energy stays volatile on Middle-East risk. Read the moves as reasons to lock supply and documentation early, not to chase a dip: Birdland's two-country production, early freight booking, material locking and full compliance paperwork are built to keep your shelves stocked and your margin steady when these indices swing.

news.title: Birdland at spoga+gafa 2026 — Hall 04.2 / C011, Cologne, 22–24 June
news.blurb: Our team is exhibiting at spoga+gafa 2026 (Koelnmesse, Cologne, 22–24 June 2026) — find us at Hall 04.2, Stand C011. Cologne is expected warm and mostly dry those days (around 24–27°C by day, light winds). To arrange a meeting, please contact our representatives Nancy or Jason. We warmly welcome partners to visit our booth.

news.title: EUDR readiness: how we document timber for EU buyers
news.blurb: What geolocation and legal-timber evidence we prepare so wood-handled lines clear from 30 Dec 2026.

news.title: Two countries, one standard: how we keep supply moving
news.blurb: A look at the Taiwan + China model that lets orders shift when a route or tariff is hit.

news.title: New for the season: the Fieldwork hand-tool range
news.blurb: Fruit pickers, grape knives, seeders, leaf rakes, litter pickers and long-reach pruners.
